title: Coming Full Circle
summary: Tribal program helps put former Nikiski High School student on a good path In the Ts’ilq’u Circle, participants may speak when they are holding the designated talking piece. Some choose the eagle feather, others choose a different item. It’s about 5:30 p.m. on a Tuesday in March, and Nico Castro finally has a chance to relax. Life is busy for this 18-year-old living in Anchorage. When he isn’t working at the mechanic shop – like he did this day, from 7 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. – you can find him on campus at the University of Alaska-Anchorage, where he...

title: Building Bridges and Computers
summary: Alaska Native Science and Engineering Program is serious fun Blaine Hayes says he learned a lot during his time at the Alaska Native Science and Engineering Program’s Middle School Academy (ANSEP) at the University of Alaska-Anchorage. title: Thrill of the Hunt
summary: Easter party has kids hopping Riley Mosquito scrambles with other kids to find eggs on the playground at Mountain View Elementary School. Rosy-cheeked and catching her breath, 9-year-old Alyssa Anderson dropped to a knee and set down the bucket of plastic eggs. title: Shadowing the Pros
summary: Students walk a day in the shoes of Dena’ina Wellness Center employees Hannah Phillips and Jessica Segura learn about health careers this morning at the Dena’ina Wellness Center during the Kenai Chamber of Commerce Job Shadow Day for Kenai Central High School students. title: New Tyotkas Elder Center Opens Doors for Service
summary: Tauna Reese serves Laura and Davis Hobson lunch in the new building.
